reasoning: Query asks for Tohoku cherry blossom spots (not Tokyo). Tool fetches only Akita, giving one prefecture of six in Tohoku. Kakunodate武家屋敷 has sakura_meisho_100 and a weeping cherry description — genuinely useful — but coverage of the broader Tohoku region (Hirosaki, Hanamiyama, Kitakami) is absent. Constraint 'not Tokyo' is trivially satisfied; the sub-region scope is not.
missing_data: Spots from Aomori (Hirosaki Park), Iwate (Kitakami Tenshochi), Miyagi, Yamagata, and Fukushima — the rest of Tohoku. A regional query should span all six prefectures.
improvement_hint: For a region-level query like Tohoku cherry blossoms, fan out get_spots across all six Tohoku prefectures and filter by sakura_meisho_100 kind before returning. Alternatively, add a region parameter to get_spots.

reasoning: Tool correctly retrieves Niigata specialty food/sake data. The response lists 24 items including the celebrated 越の三梅 trio (越乃寒梅, 八海山, 久保田), each with Wikidata QIDs, brewery origins, and source URLs. Descriptions are accurate and informative. Minor gap: response mixes in MAFF GI agricultural products (Kurosaki Chamame edamame) which are unrelated to sake, slightly reducing practical focus.
missing_data: Brewery addresses or visit info (e.g., tasting rooms) would improve trip-planning utility. Some famous brands like 〆張鶴 or 鶴齢 may be absent.
improvement_hint: Add a category=sake or kind_tags filter option to get_local_specialty so agents can request only sake-related items without agricultural GIs appearing in the same response.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts WITH hot springs in Hakuba. The tool returns Hakuba Happo-One (ski resort confirmed) but no onsen entries appear in the truncated response. The combined ski+onsen constraint is not satisfied. The municipal scrape entry about UN Tourism Village recognition is noise. Hakuba Goryu Alpine Garden and Hakuba Ōike are present but irrelevant to the query.
missing_data: Onsen facilities in Hakuba: Mimizuku no Yu, Hakuba Happo-One onsen, Kashimayari Ski Resort onsen, and Tsugaike. No onsen kind tagged results visible.
improvement_hint: Tag spots that have co-located onsen facilities (kind: onsen OR description mentions 温泉) so a dual ski+onsen query can be satisfied by a single get_spots call. Alternatively, enable multi-kind filtering.

reasoning: Query is specifically about autumn foliage (紅葉) spots in Kansai. Tool fetches generic Kyoto spots. The results show Nijō Castle, Tō-ji, and Sanzen-in (the latter genuinely famous for autumn leaves) but the selection isn't filtered by foliage relevance. No momiji kind or seasonal tag is present. Agent cannot distinguish foliage-famous spots from random Kyoto attractions without additional filtering.
missing_data: Foliage-tagged spots: Eikan-do, Tofuku-ji, Arashiyama (in Kyoto); and Kansai-wide coverage (Osaka, Nara, Hyogo). Query scope exceeds single-prefecture Kyoto.
improvement_hint: Add a momiji/foliage kind tag to relevant spots (Sanzen-in, Eikan-do, Tofuku-ji etc.) so agents can filter by season. Support a region-level query spanning multiple Kansai prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks for famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai (Arabic). Tool fetches Nara spots and the top result is Yoshino-yama with sakura_meisho_100 and prominence 1.1 — Japan's most celebrated cherry blossom site. Kōfuku-ji and other Nara parks also bloom well. Coverage is Nara-only (single Kansai prefecture), missing Osaka Castle, Himeji Castle, and Kyoto spots, but the Yoshino hit alone is an excellent answer to the core intent.
missing_data: Osaka, Kyoto, and Hyogo cherry blossom sites for full Kansai coverage. Query implied the whole region, not just Nara.
improvement_hint: Extend Kansai cherry blossom queries to fan out across at least Osaka, Nara, and Kyoto prefectures, filtering by sakura_meisho_100 kind. A region alias would avoid needing separate per-prefecture calls.

reasoning: Query asks for remote Okinawa islands with beautiful stargazing. The tool returns generic Okinawa prefecture spots: Nakagusuku Castle (Okinawa main island), Tamaudun (Naha), Shikina-en (Naha), and Taketomi Island. No stargazing or dark-sky kind tags exist. There is no data to answer the core intent. Taketomi appears but without any stargazing context. The response cannot equip an agent to answer this query.
missing_data: Stargazing spots on Iriomote, Yonaguni, Taketomi, Kume, or Miyako islands. Dark sky/星空 kind tags are absent from the corpus entirely.
improvement_hint: Add a star_gazing or dark_sky kind tag to remote islands known for low light pollution (Iriomote, Yonaguni, Taketomi). Scrape prefecture tourism pages for 星空 content and tag accordingly.

reasoning: Query asks for rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido (Thai). Tool returns get_hotels(Hokkaido) which surfaces multiple 日本秘湯を守る会 (Nihon Hitou) confirmed onsen ryokan with rural addresses: Shiranai Onsen Ryokan (Kikonai), Ginkonyu (Yakumo), Ryounkaku (Kamifurano/Tokachidake), Yukomoso (Asahidake). These are definitionally rural hidden hot spring inns. The ANA Holiday Inn Sapporo entry is irrelevant urban noise but does not undermine the rural hits.
missing_data: English names for most ryokan are null; coordinates are missing for all hitoyu-kai entries, reducing navigability.
improvement_hint: Enrich hitoyu-kai ryokan records with geocoordinates (available from the hitou.or.jp detail pages) and English names to improve trip-planning usability for international travelers.

reasoning: Query asks for Seto Inland Sea islands (瀬戸内 島々). Tool fetches Kagawa spots showing Ritsurin Garden (famous garden on mainland Takamatsu), Mannō Lake, and Marugame/Takamatsu castles. Islands relevant to the Seto Inland Sea — Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima, Megijima — do not appear in the truncated response. The mainland-heavy result set fails the island constraint.
missing_data: Naoshima (art island), Shodoshima (olive island), Teshima, Ogijima, Inujima. These Kagawa islands are Seto Inland Sea's primary draw.
improvement_hint: Index island-kind spots with an inland_sea_island tag and ensure Naoshima and Shodoshima (which have Wikidata entries) appear prominently in Kagawa get_spots results filtered by kind:island.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Tool returns 3 METI craft items: Awa Shijira Weave (indigo weave), Otani Ware (unrelated ceramics), and Awa Washi (indigo paper). The indigo connection is present but the response lists craft designations, not workshop venues or experiences. An agent cannot direct a traveler to where to do indigo dyeing from this data. see_also_wikidata_venues shows the Castle Museum but not dyeing workshops.
missing_data: Workshop venues: Awa Ai (Aizome Center) in Tokushima, specific studios offering indigo dyeing experiences. The response gives craft designations but not experiential tourism locations.
improvement_hint: Scrape Tokushima tourism pages for aizome workshop listings and add them as spot entries with a kind:craft_workshop tag, linking to the associated METI craft designation.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for shukubo (temple lodging) on Mount Koya in German. Tool returns get_hotels(Wakayama) and the first four results are confirmed Koyasan Shukubo Association members: Annyoin, Ichijoin, Ekoin, Kitamuroin — all in 高野町, all with phone numbers and source URLs. Rich Japanese descriptions of temple atmosphere, shojin ryori, and meditation. This is a near-perfect response to a very specific constraint.
missing_data: English names and English descriptions for German-speaking users. Coordinates are missing for all shukubo entries.
improvement_hint: Add English translations of shukubo descriptions (or at minimum a short summary) to help non-Japanese-speaking users. Geocoordinates from Koyasan Shukubo Association pages would improve map-based UX.

reasoning: Query asks for off-the-beaten-path samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area returns 146 results including Buke Yashiki (Shimane), Uchiyama Family Residence (Fukui), Kakunodate (Akita), and Chiran Samurai Residences (Kagoshima) — all outside Kyoto, multiple with IPDT heritage designations. Coverage is broad and geographically diverse. The 'off-the-beaten-path' nuance isn't directly scored by the server (appropriately, per editorial policy), leaving that judgment to the agent.
missing_data: Kitsuki (Oita) buke-yashiki district, Izushi (Hyogo) samurai streets are likely in the 146 but may be further down. No descriptions for some entries.
improvement_hint: Surface prominence_score in search results more clearly so agents can differentiate well-known (Kakunodate) from genuinely obscure samurai districts. Consider adding an off-peak or low_traffic tag derived from visitor stats where available.

reasoning: Query asks for summer (natsu) festivals in Akita. Tool returns get_festivals(Akita) with only 3 items — all cultural heritage performing arts (Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shrine Festival). The iconic Akita summer festivals — Kanto Festival (竿灯まつり), Noshiro Float Festival, and Omagari Fireworks — are absent. The data sources (bunka_intangible) do not capture festival events. The date field is null or historic (1705). An agent using this cannot answer the summer festival query.
missing_data: Kanto Matsuri (Aug 3-6, Akita City), Omagari Hanabi, Noshiro Neburi Nagashi. These flagship summer matsuri are not in the corpus.
improvement_hint: Supplement get_festivals with the scraped_schema_event layer for Akita (as done for Niigata in L2-27). The Akita tourism portal likely has structured event data for the major summer festivals.

reasoning: Query asks for small fishing port towns in the Sanin region (Korean). Tool returns general Shimane spots: Lake Shinji (scenic lake), Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan (silver mine, UNESCO WHS), and Mt. Sanbe. None of these are fishing port towns. The constraint 漁港 (fishing port) / 港町 (port town) is completely unaddressed. The response has high-quality data but mismatches the intent entirely.
missing_data: Fishing port towns: Mihonoseki (三保ノ関), Yunotsu (温泉津) — already has walking_road_500, Oki Islands fishing communities. No fishing_port or minato kind tags exist.
improvement_hint: Add kind tags for fishing_port and minato_machi to qualifying Shimane/Tottori coastal spots. Consider scraping Sanin fishing village tourism pages to populate these entries.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi papermaking in Gifu. Tool returns 8 Gifu craft items. Mino Washi (美濃和紙) should be present as one of Japan's most famous washi traditions in Gifu, but the visible items are Mino Ware (ceramics), Hida Shunkei (lacquer), and woodwork. The washi entries may exist but are not visible in the truncated 4000-char response. Even if present, the response gives craft designations only — not workshop/experience venues.
missing_data: Mino Washi workshop venues in Mino City (e.g., Mino Washi Museum, akari art workshops). Experience-oriented tourism data is absent from the craft designation layer.
improvement_hint: Add craft_workshop venue records for Mino Washi linked to the METI designation, and ensure washi items surface before pottery/lacquer when the query is washi-specific. Consider a keyword filter in get_local_specialty.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula (Spanish). Tool returns general Ishikawa spots dominated by Kanazawa City attractions (Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Bed and Craft in Kanazawa) plus Nanao Castle. Noto Peninsula fishing villages — Wajima, Suzu, Monzen, Sosogi — do not appear in the truncated response. The geographic sub-region constraint (Noto vs. Kanazawa) is ignored by a prefecture-wide tool call.
missing_data: Wajima (輪島) fishing port and Wajima Asaichi morning market, Suzu Cape, Noto Satoyama Satoumi area, Sosogi coast. Post-earthquake recovery sites should also be noted.
improvement_hint: Add municipality-level filtering for Noto Peninsula municipalities (Wajima, Suzu, Noto, Anamizu) or add a noto_peninsula geographic tag. The Bed and Craft entry incorrectly shows price_band:free for a hotel — needs correction.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. Tool uses search_area(砂丘) and returns 90 results. Tottori Sand Dunes appear first (expected), followed by Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari Sand Dune Museum (Hokkaido), Fukiage-hama (Kagoshima), Tsujidō Beach (Kanagawa), and a Kyoto mountain. The 'not just Tottori' intent is well served with multi-prefecture coverage. Fukiage-hama as a nagisa_100 dune coast is a genuine lesser-known recommendation.
missing_data: Sakata Sand Dunes (Chiba), Nakatajima Sand Dune (Shizuoka — Japan's 3 great dunes), Uchimaru Sand Dunes. A few prominent ones may be in the 90 results but not in the 4000-char window.
wrong_data: The Ishikari entry is a museum about sand dunes, not a sand dune itself — subtle but relevant distinction for a visitor.
improvement_hint: Distinguish sand_dune feature entries from museums-about-sand-dunes in kind tagging. Surface the three nationally renowned dunes (Tottori, Nakatajima, Sakata) as a top tier before the long tail.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional crafts in the Sanin region in Chinese. Tool returns Shimane crafts with Chinese translations: Iwami Ware (石见烧), Iwami Washi (石州和纸), Unshuu Abacus (云州算盘), and Izumo Stone Lantern (出云石灯笼). All 4 items have high-confidence Chinese descriptions, METI designations, and source URLs. The language constraint is perfectly satisfied. Coverage is Shimane only (Tottori missing from Sanin), but Shimane crafts alone answer the query meaningfully.
missing_data: Tottori crafts (Inshuu Washi, Tottori mingei) for complete Sanin coverage. The see_also_wikidata_venues section adds Iwami Ginzan as context.
improvement_hint: Add a sanin region alias that combines Shimane and Tottori results. Also consider including Tottori's METI crafts in a Sanin-scoped query.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. The tool returns general Hokkaido spots: Shōwa-shinzan (volcano), Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle, and Daisetsuzan. No lavender field data exists in the corpus. The famous Furano/Biei lavender farms, and alternatives like Nakafurano or Hokuryu Town sunflower/lavender are absent. The response is completely unable to address the query intent.
missing_data: Lavender fields: Farm Tomita (Furano), Nakafurano Lavender Fields, Hokuryu Sunflower Village. A lavender/seasonal_flower kind tag does not exist in the corpus.
improvement_hint: Add a seasonal_flower or hana_batake kind tag to applicable Hokkaido spots (Farm Tomita, Shikisai-no-Oka, etc.) and scrape Furano/Biei tourism pages to populate lavender field entries with bloom season metadata.

reasoning: Query in Tagalog asks for traditional castle towns (jokamachi) in Kyushu. Tool fetches Oita prefecture spots showing individual castles (Usuki, Nanao-equivalent Oka Castle, Usa Shrine) but not jokamachi districts. Query intent is castle towns (preserved historic townscapes around castles), not just castles. Also, the scope is all of Kyushu but only Oita is queried. Hita (Oita) and Yanagawa (Fukuoka) are notable castle towns absent from results.
missing_data: Jokamachi towns: Hita (Oita), Yanagawa (Fukuoka), Chiran (Kagoshima already has buke_yashiki), Usuki Ningyozaka historic district. Other Kyushu prefectures not covered.
improvement_hint: Add jokamachi kind tag to qualifying preserved castle town districts and support a Kyushu region alias in get_spots. The distinct jokamachi intent should retrieve preservation districts, not just individual castles.

reasoning: Query asks for kominka (old farmhouse) stays in Hokuriku (Japanese). Tool fetches Ishikawa hotels. The response shows two hitoyu-kai onsen ryokan (mountain hot spring inns), then 'Student housing 1' (OSM noise), then AB Hotel (business hotel). The Bed and Craft kominka hotel network (visible in L2-20 data as id Q126788301-bedcraft) may exist but is not shown in get_hotels results. Kominka_stay type properties are not surfacing. The query constraint is unmet.
missing_data: Kominka stay properties in Ishikawa, Toyama, Fukui. Bed and Craft (Inami, Toyama) is the most prominent network but doesn't appear here. Fukui satoyama kominka lodges also absent.
wrong_data: 'Student housing 1' is irrelevant OSM noise that should be filtered from accommodation results.
improvement_hint: Filter out OSM entries where lodging_type is 'hostel' with null name or student_housing type from tourism-facing hotel results. Ensure kominka_stay type records surface when the query is for traditional farmhouse lodging.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines in the Kii Peninsula. Tool returns get_japan_heritage(Wakayama) and the top canonical heritage entries include Kongōbu-ji (Mt. Koya, UNESCO), Kumano Kodō (pilgrimage routes, UNESCO WHS), and Kumano Sanzan (three grand shrines). The tool_scope_note correctly steers agents toward UNESCO/pilgrimage content. Kongōbu-ji description explicitly mentions Mt. Koya. This is an excellent response covering the query comprehensively with authoritative heritage data.
missing_data: Nachi Taisha and its waterfall (could be individually listed). Hayatama Taisha and Hongu Taisha as individual entries. These are in Kumano Sanzan aggregate but individual listings would help.
improvement_hint: List the three Kumano Sanzan shrines individually (Hongu, Nachi, Hayatama) as distinct entries in addition to the aggregate, enabling agents to provide specific coordinates and visiting details for each.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast. Tool returns 129 Niigata festival events. Nagaoka Fireworks (長岡まつり大花火大会, Aug 2-3) is present and is Japan's most famous fireworks on the Sea of Japan coast. However it appears third in the list, after spring flower events (芝桜まつり, ヒメサユリ祭り) which are irrelevant. The fireworks constraint requires filtering; all 129 events are returned without ranking by query relevance. Sea of Japan coast framing is Niigata-only.
missing_data: Omagari Fireworks (Akita), Wajima Taisai fireworks (Ishikawa), other Sea of Japan coast prefectures. The query scope is coastwide, not just Niigata.
improvement_hint: Add event_type or keyword filtering to get_festivals so agents can pass q='花火' to surface fireworks events first. Ranking by event prominence or hanabi kind tag would surface Nagaoka Fireworks at position 1.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. Tool returns only 2 items: Hakata Ori (silk weaving, not bamboo — listed because Oita is a minor co-production area for Fukuoka) and Beppu Bamboo Crafts (genuinely relevant). The bamboo craft Beppu Chiku is present and well-described with materials and techniques. But Hakata Ori dominates position 1 despite being primarily a Fukuoka craft. Coverage is thin (2 items). Missing: visit information for Beppu bamboo workshops.
missing_data: Beppu Bamboo Craft Museum address/visit info. Any other Oita bamboo craft variants. The response does not include experiential visit options.
improvement_hint: When production_area_text shows a cross-prefecture craft, rank items where the queried prefecture is the primary production area higher. Beppu Bamboo Crafts should be ranked first for an Oita-specific query.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Tool returns Ehime prefecture spots. Ōmishima (island in Seto Inland Sea) has cycling_route kind and is on the Shimanami Kaido route — a genuinely excellent cycling hit. However the truncated response is dominated by castles (Ōzu, Yuzuki, Uwajima) rather than cycling infrastructure. Coverage is Ehime-only; rural Shikoku cycling also spans Kochi (Shimanto River), Tokushima, and Kagawa. The Shimanami Kaido itself may be in the data.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaido route explicitly, Shimanto River cycling (Kochi), Kagawa cycling routes. Other three Shikoku prefectures absent from query scope.
improvement_hint: Ensure cycling_route kind spots are prioritized in results when the query intent is cycling. Add Shimanami Kaido as a dedicated entry with route details. Support a Shikoku region query spanning all four prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto — a venue/location query. Tool returns get_traditional_arts() which lists 183 items covering cultural heritage performing arts broadly. Kabuki appears as UNESCO ICH entry (QID Q199701) with a brief description but no venue data, no location, no theater names. The constraint 'outside Tokyo and Kyoto' cannot be evaluated. An agent using this response cannot direct a user to a specific kabuki venue.
missing_data: Kabuki theater venues outside major cities: Uchiko-za (Ehime), Kanamaru-za (Kagawa), Eirakukan (Hyogo) — rural kabuki theaters. These venue-level entities are absent from the corpus.
improvement_hint: Add venue entities for historic rural kabuki theaters (Kanamaru-za, Uchiko-za, Eirakukan) as spots with kind:kabuki_theater and link them to the UNESCO kabuki ICH entry via a related_designation field.
